coin_profile.name: Ethereum
coin_profile.description: Ethereum is the leading smart contract platform and the foundation of decentralised finance (DeFi). It transitioned to proof-of-stake in 2022 (The Merge), making it energy-efficient. ETH is used to pay for computation on the network and is deflationary post-EIP-1559, with a portion of fees burned with every transaction. It underpins the majority of DeFi, NFT, and Layer 2 ecosystems.

ETH.name: Ethereum

ETH.iv_method: M1 DCF (fees), M2 P/S analog, M3 P/E analog, M+ NVT, M+ Peer value

bull_points: Protocol generates $0.13B in annual fees — demonstrating real, sustained user demand
Multi-year on-chain data available — protocol has an established track record of fee generation across market cycles
Top-2 by market cap — established network effect and deep liquidity across major exchanges
bear_points: DCF model suggests Ethereum is trading at a 34493.4% premium to fair value (est. $6.18)
High MC/Fees ratio of 1999.66x means investors are paying a large premium relative to actual protocol fee generation
FDV/Holders Revenue of 12817.75x — very little fee revenue currently reaches token holders, limiting investment returns

coin_profile.name: Ethereum Classic
coin_profile.description: Ethereum Classic is the original Ethereum chain that continued after the 2016 DAO hack fork. It maintains the original 'code is law' philosophy and is proof-of-work. ETC has a fixed supply schedule and positions itself as a store of value for the Ethereum ecosystem.

ETC.name: Ethereum Classic

coin_profile.name: Ethena
coin_profile.description: Ethena is an RWA-backed synthetic dollar protocol. USDe is Ethena's stablecoin, backed by a delta-neutral strategy using staked ETH and short perpetual positions. The protocol distributes yield from this strategy to sUSDe holders and ENA stakers.

ENA.name: Ethena
